title: NOC 64410 – Security guards and related security service occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Security guards and related security service professionals under NOC 64410 are the frontline defenders of Canada’s private and public infrastructure. From patrolling corporate campuses and monitoring high-tech surveillance systems to ensuring safety at international airports, these TEER 4 professionals are the primary deterrent against theft, vandalism, and safety breaches. This is one of the most accessible and high-demand roles for new immigrants, offering a fast route to stable employment while pursuing a long-term path to Permanent Residency (PR).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group644 – Customer and information services representativesMinor Group6441 – Security guards and related security service occupationsNOC Code64410

duties: Control access to establishments, maintain order, and enforce regulations at public events and within establishments.
Perform security checks of passengers and luggage at airports using electronic equipment.
Drive and guard armoured cars and pick up/deliver cash and valuables to banks or retail establishments.
Patrol assigned areas on foot or in vehicles to prevent and detect signs of intrusion and ensure security of doors, windows, and gates.
Monitor closed-circuit television (CCTV) and alarm systems to detect suspicious activities.
Respond to fire alarms and assist in the evacuation of buildings during emergencies.
Prepare reports on incidents, property damage, or theft for management and law enforcement.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Provincial Security Guard License: Mandatory in all Canadian provinces. This requires the completion of a basic security training course and passing a provincial exam.
Clear Criminal Record: A clean background check is a prerequisite for licensing.
First Aid/CPR Certification: Most employers require a valid Basic or Emergency First Aid certificate.
Driver’s License: Often required for mobile patrol positions.
Firearms License (PAL): Specifically required for armoured car guards or specialized high-risk roles.
job_outlook: The outlook for security services is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), over 35,000 new job openings are expected through 2031. This growth is fueled by the increased outsourcing of security functions by both government and private sectors.
labour_market_demand: The demand for security guards in Canada is currently Very Good. As urban centers expand and corporate security needs become more sophisticated, the requirement for licensed guards has reached record levels. In 2026, major demand is seen in Ontario (Greater Toronto Area), British Columbia (Metro Vancouver), and Alberta (Calgary/Edmonton). The growth of high-value logistics and the expansion of public transit systems are also driving significant recruitment.
High Accessibility: With a relatively short licensing process, you can begin working in your field shortly after arrival.
Diverse Work Environments: Choose from roles in corporate retail, industrial sites, airports, or mobile patrol.
Clear Career Progression: Gain experience to move into Security Supervision (NOC 62029) or specialized roles in Private Investigation.
Flexible Schedules: Many security roles offer 24/7 shift rotations, ideal for balancing other commitments.
pathways: BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: Specifically for those working in the service sector (requires 9 months of work in BC).
Alberta Opportunity Stream: A fast-track for those already working in Alberta in this category with a valid job offer.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for TEER 4 workers with job offers in the Atlantic provinces.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Targeted at specific service roles that support Ontario’s labor market infrastructure.
